rulings.Case Title: BAYSIDE LASSEN, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY VS. ASIAN, INC., A CALIFORNIA NONPROFIT PUBLIC BENEFIT ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 7, 2025 line 2. DEFENDANT LASSEN HOUSING ASSOCIATES, L.P., A CALIFORNIA LIMITED PARTNERSHIP (FROM CONSOLIDATED CASE CGC-23-610514), BAYSIDE LASSEN, LLC., A DELAWARE LIMITED DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained as to the cause of action for quiet title for Asian, Inc to include the legal description of the property and properly verify its complaint. Demurrer is also sustained with leave to amend as to the cause of action for breach of contract against Lassen Housing Associates, LP to allege a contract to which this entity is a party, and how this party breached said contract. Asian, INC shall have 10 days from notice of entry of order to file an amended complaint. Demurrer is otherwise overruled, causes of action are sufficiently stated and are not barred by statutes of limitations (based on the allegations made in the First Amended Complaint and without making any factual determinations, which would not be appropriate on a demurrer). =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: ADAM FAGEL ET AL VS. OYVIND SEAN KINSEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 7, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT OYVIND KINSEY DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained as to causes of action 3, 9, 10 and 11 based on 1-year statute of limitations. CCP 340(a). Demurrer to the 12th cause of action is overruled. Punitive damages and penalty are different. See Beeman v. rulings.Case Title: ADAM FAGEL ET AL VS. OYVIND SEAN KINSEY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 7, 2025 line 4. DEFENDANT OYVIND KINSEY MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part; DENIED in part; and moot in part. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to references and request for damages under CC 1950 as this statute does not appear to apply to the facts of this case. Motion is granted with leave to amend as to the request for attorney's fees for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting basis for attorney's fees claim. Motion is granted with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege facts supporting award of quintuple damages. Motion is denied as to the request for punitive damages, which are sufficiently supported by factual allegations. Motion is otherwise moot. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: ANDREW KING VS. THEODORE J. GUARRIELLO JR. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Expunge Lis Pendens; Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Plaintiff And His Counsel; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Declarations Of Chris Boulter And Jonathan D. Fink
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for January 7, 2025 line 6. DEFENDANT THEODORE GUARRIELLO , TRUSTEE OF THE THEODORE J. GUARRIELLO, JR. REVOCABLE TRUST DATED 6/18/2003, AND RESTATED 6/29/2006, BRET GREENBERG, TIMOTHY J. SPAETH AND ANDREA N. SPAETH, TRUSTEES, PACIFIC PREMIER TRUST, CUSTODIAN FBO BRET, VAL-CHRIS INVESTMENTS, INC., PACIFIC PREMIER TRUST, CUSTODIAN FBO BRUCE BUCKLEY, TJ GUARRIELLO, JR. FAMILY FOUNDATION, BRUCE NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION TO EXPUNGE LIS PENDENS; REQUEST FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS AGAINST PLAINTIFF AND HIS COUNSEL; MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES; DECLARATIONS OF CHRIS BOULTER AND JONATHAN D. FINK is GRANTED. This motion has been previously continued from December 11, 2024 calendar for supplemental briefing to address compliance with CCP 405.22 as the record did not indicate any filing of the Notice of Lis Pendens, let alone a filing "immediately following recordation." In the supplemental declaration Plaintiff admitted that no such filing had been made and argued that failure to comply with CCP 405.22 does not render the Lis Pendens defective. Plaintiff has not filed a notice of lis pendens with the Court. The lis pendens is now expunged as Plaintiff failed to show probable validity of a real property claim. Defendants' requests for fees to be paid by the Plaintiff within 30 days of notice of entry of order is granted in the amount of $7,046.50. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (669) 254-5252; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified and the opposing party does not appear.
